1. You could lose all the money you invest
The performance of most cryptoassets can be highly volatile, with their value dropping as quickly as it can rise. You should be prepared to lose all the money you invest in cryptoassets.
The cryptoasset market is largely unregulated. There is a risk of losing money or any cryptoassets you purchase due to risks such as cyber-attacks, financial crime, and firm failure.
2. You should not expect to be protected if something goes wrong

The Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) doesn’t protect this type of investment because it’s not a ‘specified investment’ under the UK regulatory regime – in other words, this type of investment isn’t recognised as the sort of investment that the FSCS can protect. Learn more by using the FSCS investment protection checker here.
The Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS) will not be able to consider complaints related to this firm. Learn more about FOS protection here.
3. You may not be able to sell your investment when you want to

There is no guarantee that investments in cryptoassets can be easily sold at any given time. The ability to sell a cryptoasset depends on various factors, including the supply and demand in the market at that time.
Operational failings such as technology outages, cyber-attacks, and comingling of funds could cause unwanted delay and you may be unable to sell your cryptoassets at the time you want.
Investments in cryptoassets can be complex, making it difficult to understand the risks associated with the investment.
You should do your own research before investing. If something sounds too good to be true, it probably is.
5. Don’t put all your eggs in one basket
Putting all your money into a single type of investment is risky. Spreading your money across different investments makes you less dependent on any one to do well.
A good rule of thumb is not to invest more than 10% of your money in high-risk investments.
Approved by Gateway 21 Limited on Sep 23, 2024.
Due to the potential for losses, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) considers this investment to be high risk.
For further information about cryptoassets, visit the FCA’s website here.
If you are interested in learning more about how to protect yourself, visit the FCA’s website here.
Price changes are for the past 24h
Past performance isn\'t a reliable indicator of future events. Source: OKX. Price changes are for the past 24h.
Certain tokens may rely on assets held with third parties which may or may not be verifiable or visible to the token holder. Legal recourse to any pledged assets may be limited.

Decentralised crypto projects can have founders / foundation / developers who may exert undue influence on the project outcome. These founders / foundation / developers may be anonymous which can lead to an increase in enterprise risk. Disagreements within the Founders / Foundation / Community can lead to events such as forks or dilution which could lead to diminishing the value of previously established assets. Network validators may prioritize their own interests, which may not always align with the overall health or decentralization of the network.
Certain tokens may use a energy-intensive consensus mechanism, raising environmental concerns about its carbon footprint.
The SEC has alleged that certain tokens are a security. A legal ruling against such a token in the USA could affect token liquidity and/or value.

Borrowing/Lending is high risk and speculative and offers leverage to magnify trading risks and returns. Margin call requirements may be made quickly or frequently, especially in times of high volatility and if participants cannot meet them, their positions may be closed out and any shortfall may be borne by that participant. This risk is not directly borne by the platform operating token holder.
The crypto space has been associated with various fraudulent schemes, including rug pulls, fake initial coin offerings (ICOs), phishing attacks.
DeFi protocols frequently depend on external data sources or oracles, and any tampering or inaccuracies in these data streams can result in a lack of trust and reliability in the protocols.
Please review the following risks which may apply to certain cryptoassets listed on the OKX exchange.

Used to verify general account activities and authorize DEX trades.

A passkey is activated on a network the first time it is used to authenticate a DEX trade.

Once activated, the passkey can automatically be used to authenticate future trades. You won’t need to reactivate it for this network.

You can also check a network’s passkey activation status on this page at any time.

A passkey is activated on a network the first time it’s used to authenticate a Pay transaction. The one-time activation may include a small network fee.

After that, the passkey can be used on the network without additional fees.

You can also check a network’s passkey activation status on this page at any time.

A passkey is a secure authentication method that lets you verify your identity using your face, fingerprint, or device PIN instead of a password.
Passkeys replace passwords with an authentication method that is linked to your unique device and biometrics, making your account more secure.
Passkeys can prevent phishing and scams. Verify your identity using your face, fingerprint, or device PIN — no passwords needed.
How do passkeys keep your account safe?

Set how often the AI analyzes the market. Shorter intervals suit fast-paced strategies, while longer intervals offer better stability for long-term positions.

Display the remaining time of the last candlestick.
Display the price scale (y-axis) on the chart.
Time is represented on the x-axis, while prices are on the y-axis. Each candle is made up of the body and the wicks.

The body of the candle represents the price difference between the open and close prices during the candle\'s timeframe. The top of the upper wick and the bottom of the lower wick indicate the highest and lowest prices the crypto hit during the timeframe.
Unlike stock markets, crypto markets never close. The “open” and “close” prices are the prices at the beginning and end of the selected timeframe.
Candlesticks show the direction and range of a market\'s price movements.
The timeframe defines the period of time each candle represents. Users can set timeframes ranging from 1 second to 3 months.
You can zoom out using the pinch gesture to view the price trend over time.
Your open limit or stop orders will appear on the chart, including the order type, quantity, and price.
In futures and margin trading, you can view the average cost of your positions on the chart.
You can view your historical orders on the chart. “B” indicates executed buy orders and is displayed under a candle, while “S” indicates executed sell orders and is dislayed above a candle.
Countdown displays the remaining time of the last candle on the chart.
To change the chart theme, tap More > Theme.
To switch between Original and TradingView charts, tap More > Chart display.

Unlock the secrets to trading success

This indicator reflects the percentage of long vs short positions held by the top 100 traders. One active account is entitled to one vote, regardless of its position size. (Only the accounts that are holding positions are counted as active.)

This indicator shows the average number of contracts for different sets grouped by their individual number of contracts at a specific time. The area of circle is proportional to the number of contracts in that set

This indicator shows the ratio of the total number of long vs short positions over a given period of time. The data includes the total number of positions of weekly, bi-weekly and quarterly futures and perpetual swap contracts (long short directions is calculated based on the user\'s net position of that token)
This indicator shows the estimated fee rate of the perpetual swap underlying asset over a given period of time. The estimated fee rate = the current fee rate when fee is collected.

Buy Taker Volume: The trading volume of active buy orders within that period (taker buy orders), which is the amount of capital inflow\nSell Taker Volume: The trading volume of active sell orders within that period (take sell orders), which is the amount of capital outflow.
This indicator shows the changes in spot index price and futures price and their differences at the same time. The basis of a specific time = futures price - spot index price.

Open Interest: The sum of long and short holding of futures and perpetual swap of a specific asset at a specific time.\nTrading volume: The total trading volume of futures and perpetual swap of a specific asset within a specific period of time.

This indicator reflects how the top 100 traders use their deposited funds. Unlike TTSI, this indicator doesn\'t count votes. Instead, it is calculated from the average percentage of initial margin used by the top traders.

If you choose to reserve required funds, the total amount for this bot will be frozen in advance.\n\nIf unchecked, only the funds for the initial and first safety orders are frozen. Ensure sufficient funds are in your account for subsequent safety orders; otherwise, these orders will fail.
Overall price range of safety orders ({percentage}) exceeds 100%. Decrease the marked parameters accordingly.
Stop loss target is less than the overall price range of safety orders ({percentage}). Increase the SL PnL% or decrease safety order parameters accordingly.
Insufficient available balance. Deposit or transfer more funds.
Funds not reserved. There’s a chance future safety orders may fail.
Total amount of funds that may potentially be transferred to DCA bot for inactive safety orders that are far away from current price (Only applicable without Reserved required fund).
Maximum amount to be used by this trading bot

Applied to the investment amount for placing the next safety order. The multiplier affects the average holding price of executed crypto and determines the overall investment strategy.\n\nFor example, with a safety order amount = 100 USDT, multiplier = 2. Safety orders will be placed with amount 100 = (2^0 × 100), 200 = (2^1 × 100), 400 = (2^2 × 100), etc.
The ratio of the first safety order to the initial order amount.
First order initiated by the DCA bot at the start of each trading cycle
The maximum number of safety orders that can be placed in a trading cycle. Actual number of placed safety orders may be determined by your funds situation.
Percentage difference between two consecutive safety orders, denominated in quote currency

Used to calculate the order price for the next safety order. The multiplier affects the average holding price of executed crypto and determines the overall investment strategy.\n\nFor example, with a price step = 1%, multiplier = 2. Safety orders will be placed when the price changes 1% = (0 + 1 × 2^0)%, 3% = (1 + 1 × 2^1)%, 7% = (3 + 1 × 2^2)%, etc.
Safety orders will trade incrementally in each trading cycle, to lower the average cost of the trading pairs being traded, and help to achieve the take profit target faster.
Notice: Trigger threshold is generally set below {tholdNum}

{termRsi} is a momentum indicator that gauges the speed of price movements. Generally, RSI uses 30 as the oversold threshold, which describes the price of an asset in relation to its fair value. A value lower than the oversold threshold suggests the short-term decline is coming to an end and a rebound could be imminent, so it would be ideal for starting a long DCA bot.
Over a 30-day period, the number of signals generated based on the selected indicator in a given timeframe

Stop loss price = Initial order filled price × (1 – SL PnL%). When the SL price has been triggered and fully filled, the current strategy will be terminated.

Take-profit PnL% = Take-profit price / Average position cost – 1.\nWhen the take-profit price is triggered and the order is fully filled, the bot will end the current trading cycle and start the next one as specified.
The parameter is recommended based on historical market data with a 180-day backtest period.\n\nYou should not consider this data as a commitment or guarantee in live trading.

Buy crypto to lower the average cost price.

• When the price drops to your preset level, the bot triggers a buy.
• When the market rebounds and hits your take-profit target, the bot triggers a sell.

Buy in batches, sell in one go, and significantly reduce your average cost price.\n\nSimply set your dip percentage and take-profit target. Then, let the bot automatically buy low and sell high during each market dip and rebound.
You can manually stop the bot anytime.\n\nWhen stopped, all pending martingale orders are canceled. All funds are returned to your trading account — you can check the details before stopping.

By electing to use the Dual Crypto Deposit flywheel bot (DCD Bot), you understand and agree to the following:

A. Flywheel bot is not a principal-guaranteed product. The profit and loss of Flywheel bot is directly related to the parameters you set, including your prediction of the direction of the price movement, as well as the market volatility, of the underlying digital asset.
B. The trading market is volatile and may change rapidly in terms of pricing and liquidity. You are fully responsible and liable for any and all Flywheel bot trading activities on your account. When the market fluctuates dramatically, there is a risk that your bot may not be able to match suitable Flywheel products, resulting in temporary inactivity of the Flywheel bot.
C. OKX reserves the right to terminate the services in advance at its sole discretion.
D. The Flywheel bot is subject to various risk-control measures taken on the OKX platform, as detailed under the Terms of Service.
E. Holding of digital assets, including the use of the Flywheel bot, involves a high degree of risk and can fluctuate greatly. You should carefully consider whether trading such instruments such as the Flywheel bot and/or holding digital assets is suitable for you in light of your financial situation.

Ending the current cycle will close all positions, cancel open orders, and immediately start the next cycle.

End of cycle: Stop bot once the first cycle ends\n\nTriggered by price: Stop bot once the last price reaches trigger price
Specify the maximum amount that can be transferred from the trading account to the bot.

When the maintenance margin ratio reaches a high-risk threshold, the auto-transfer margin feature will automatically use funds from your trading account to prevent forced liquidation.\n\nHowever, be aware that this action could impact your other positions in the trading account. Under extreme market conditions, transferring funds from your trading account may affect the maintenance margin ratio of your main account and could lead to liquidation. In some scenarios, the maintenance margin ratio might drop too quickly to the liquidation level before the auto-transfer margin feature can be activated.
Overall price range of safety orders ({percentage}) exceeds 100%. Decrease the marked parameters accordingly.
Stop loss target is less than the overall price range of safety orders ({percentage}). Increase the SL PnL% or decrease safety order parameters accordingly.
Leverage exceeding 10x will increase the risk of forced liquidation. Trade with caution.
Insufficient available balance. Deposit or transfer more funds.
A safety order may not be fully filled when this estimated liquidation price is higher than the trigger price of any one of the following safety orders. Set the parameters with caution.
The price trigger setting takes effect from the next cycle if a cycle is currently active
Exceeds available amount in trading account
Max transfer limit can’t be empty

Applied to the investment amount for placing the next safety order. The multiplier affects the average holding price of executed crypto and determines the overall investment strategy.\n\nFor example, with a safety order amount = 100 USDT, multiplier = 2. Safety orders will be placed with amount 100 = (2^0 × 100), 200 = (2^1 × 100), 400 = (2^2 × 100), etc.
The ratio of the first safety order to the initial order amount.
Margin of the first order initiated by the DCA bot at the start of each trading cycle
The maximum number of safety orders that can be placed in a trading cycle. Actual number of placed safety orders may be determined by your margin situation.
Percentage difference between two consecutive safety orders, denominated in quote currency

Used to calculate the order price for the next safety order. The multiplier affects the average holding price of executed crypto and determines the overall investment strategy.\n\nFor example, with a price step = 1%, multiplier = 2. Safety orders will be placed when the price changes 1% = (0 + 1 × 2^0)%, 3% = (1 + 1 × 2^1)%, 7% = (3 + 1 × 2^2)%, etc.
Safety orders will trade incrementally in each trading cycle, to lower the average cost of the trading pairs being traded, and help to achieve the take profit target faster.
This modification applies to current cycle only, and the modified take profit price won’t be updated as average cost changes

{termRsi} is a momentum indicator that gauges the speed of price movements. Generally, RSI uses 30 as the oversold threshold, 70 as the overbought threshold, which describes the price of an asset in relation to its fair value. \nA value lower than the oversold threshold suggests the short-term decline is coming to an end and a rebound could be imminent, so it would be ideal for starting a long position of Futures DCA. Vice versa, higher than overbought threshold is an opportunity for opening short position.
This is the estimated liquidation price and is for reference only. The actual liquidation price depends on the average cost, floating PnL and margin of the position.
A stop-loss limit order will be submitted when the stop trigger price is attained or penetrated, which eliminates the price risk of slippage but may “miss the market” altogether.
A stop-loss market order will be submitted when the stop triggered price is attained or penetrated, which is not guaranteed a specific execution price but is likely to get filled immediately.
Notice: Overbought threshold is generally set above {tholdNum}
Notice: Trigger threshold is generally set below {tholdNum}

Long position: Stop loss price = Initial order fill price × (1 – SL PnL%)\nShort position: Stop loss price = Initial order fill price × (1 + SL PnL%)\nWhen the SL price has been triggered and fully filled, the bot will stop

Long position: Take-profit PnL% = Take-profit price / Average position cost – 1.\nShort position: Take-profit PnL% = 1 – Take-profit price / Average position cost.\nWhen the take-profit price is triggered and the order is fully filled, the bot will end the current cycle index and start the next one as specified.
Maximum amount to be used by this trading bot. Please note that for potential expenses like funding fee will be transferred upon needed directly from your trading account.
The asynchronous-mode bot duplicates the lead bot’s parameters but operates independently. Over time, this may result in discrepancies between the PnL of the lead bot and the following bot.
Your profit won’t be withdrawn. It will stay in the bot and be automatically used as margin only in the next cycle.
The synchronous-mode bot starts and ends (by taking profit) its trading cycles at the same time as the lead bot, maintaining a consistent pace between the two DCA bots.
You can return to this page from the bot homepage to continue.\n\nPlease use your voucher soon to avoid expiration.
You still have a bot loss coverage voucher. The current bot is in profit. Closing the bot now will void the voucher, and no compensation will be provided.

Suitable for medium to long-term holding, this bot involves multi-cycle repetitive arbitrage. It includes batch buying during market dips and selling once for arbitrage within each cycle, continuing automatically in a loop.
Split a one-off investment across multiple price levels. Lead to a better entry price and help you earn profits faster in a rebound or correction.
Continuously earn long-term steady returns from the cyclic crypto markets, and outperform the buy-and-hold strategy on any timeframe.
Define strategy aggressiveness with price steps and volume multiplier, or choose the popular RSI signal for the Trend as Your Friend like trading.
1. To get the best entry and exit prices
2. Gain from cycles rather than HODL
3. Earn from buying the dip or selling the rip

Buy in batches, sell in one go, and significantly reduce the cost of your futures positions.

• The bot opens a position when the price reaches your preset level.
• It closes a position when the market rebounds and hits your take-profit target.

Quickly create your bot with AI parameters.

• Price steps: Define the price movement needed to trigger the next position.
• Take-profit target: Set the profit percentage to close positions for each cycle.

Select the trading direction based on your market prediction.

• Long: Ideal for rising markets.
• Short: Ideal for declining markets.
You can manually stop the bot anytime.\n\nWhen stopped, all pending martingale orders are canceled. All funds are returned to your trading account — you can check the details before stopping.

This will copy the main settings (e.g. leverage, price range, grid quantity) to the bot setup page, but the trading direction will be reversed.\n\nNote: Extra margin must be re-added, and advanced settings (take profit, stop loss, start condition, and grid mode) need to be reset manually.

When modifying parameters, existing grid profits may be reinvested. If your order enters a high-risk state, part of the profit will also be used as margin and temporarily unavailable. Once the risk level decreases, the withheld portion will become withdrawable.
Increasing the slippage can speed up order execution but may lead to higher slippage costs.\nLowering the slippage brings the average price closer to your target but may slow down the execution.

To calculate your USDT PnL, the system first converts your positions into a total USDT cost using the {crypto} price when you opened or added to the position. It then compares this with the total USDT value based on the current price (open orders) or the close price (historical orders).\nThe difference is your converted total USDT PnL.\nNote: This figure is for reference only. Your actual returns are based on the amount of crypto you earn.
Current bot only supports opening positions on creation.
Invested amount = Initial investment + Increased positions
The taker fee is calculated using the maker fee rate of the same tier. The difference is recorded as the grid’s “Unpaired PnL.”
Due to a drop in your fee tier, some of your grids may yield negative profits.
Exceeding {warnLeverage}x leverage may increase the liquidation possibility. Trade with caution.
Due to market fluctuations and your investment amount is insufficient, the modified parameters cannot take effect.
All your existing open position will be closed at market price and a neutral futures grid will be reinitialised based on the updated settings.

Your invested amount ({invest} {ccy}), reinvested profits ({reinvest} {ccy}), and unpaired PnL ({unpairAmt} {ccy}) will combine to reinitialize your grid with the new settings. The bot may close some of your open positions at market prices.

Your invested amount ({invest} {ccy}), reinvested profits ({reinvest} {ccy}), and unpaired PnL ({unpairAmt} {ccy}) will combine to reinitialize your grid with the new settings. The bot may close some of your open positions (estimated {portionAmt} contracts) at market prices.

This bot uses a 1x crypto-margined short position to hedge price moves and pairs it with grid trading to earn steady returns. It targets grid profits, funding fees, auto-earn, and staking rewards, while reducing the impact of market swings.\n\nWith 1x leverage, liquidation risk stays low and exposure is controlled—designed to preserve value while earning multiple yields.

The more grids bot has, the higher the arbitrage frequency, but the lower the profit per arbitrage. The maximum number of grids in each price range varies, depending on per-grid profit and price precision.
You’ve reached the maximum limit for open positions at your position tier, and can’t open more positions.
The current order is close to its estimated liquidation price. To avoid forced liquidation, please adjust your margin promptly.
Go to “Trading bots > Ongoing” to view your smart arbitrage assets and order performance.

After increasing your positions, the volume of single grid trade will increase, thereby increasing the profit from each successful arbitrage.\n\nBut in volatile markets, the volume might not increase. In that case, the reserved funds stay as margin to help manage risk.

Segmented Hedging automatically buys low and sells high.\n\nWhen prices fall, it accumulates crypto; when prices rise, it hedges by selling in batches at higher levels. With 1x leverage hedging, it helps reduce liquidation risk and aims to earn grid profits, funding fees, auto-earn, and staking rewards.

Instant: Immediately starts grid trading on bot creation

Price: After created, grid bot will be activated when trigger price is reached

RSI: Starts the bot when technical indicator triggered and other conditions are fulfilled

TradingView signal: Starts the bot upon receiving the relevant custom signal from TradingView

Due to unpredictable market conditions, for long grids, the minimum investment amount for non-instant triggers can be greater than or equal to that for an instant trigger.

For short grids, non-instant triggers may fail due to insufficient margin at the time of triggering. Please be aware of the risks.
Your available balance is insufficient to cover the required margin (actual and reserved margin).

Slippage control keeps fill prices closer to your target, especially during high volatility or large trades.\n\nYou can adjust this anytime when opening or closing positions. If left unset, orders execute at the best available market price, which may vary from your expectations.
The reserved margin is low, which may increase the risk of liquidation or bot stoppage due to insufficient funds.
The estimated annualized funding fees (APR) based on funding fee trends from the past 30 days. This is a projection and does not guarantee actual future returns.

The estimated average price at which the bot sells your crypto. \n\nWhen the bot starts, it sells a portion of {crypto} immediately. As prices rise, it gradually sells the rest until the top price is reached. This figure is an estimate and may differ from the actual execution.

The estimated average price at which the bot buys back your crypto.\n\nAs prices fall, the bot gradually buys the {crypto}. If the price hits the bottom of the range, it buys back all the sold crypto. This figure is an estimate and may differ from the actual execution.
If selected, a margin will be reserved to reduce liquidation risk and support smooth bot operation. Unchecking this option allows for custom margin but may increase the risk of liquidation.
The current price is out of the grid range. The bot has suspended trading.\nIt will resume automatically when the price returns, or you can adjust your bot parameters at any time.
The current crypto price is out of the grid range. The bot has suspended trading.

When the bot’s PnL% (excluding closing fees) reaches the take-profit or falls to the stop-loss threshold, the bot will stop and close all positions at market price. Actual returns or losses determined by the realized closing position.

Max decreased amount = Accumulative manual transfers (in) after strategy created – Manual transfers (out) + Refunded commission fees + Funding received – Fees deducted – Funding deducted. The calculation considers the historical accumulative transfers to avoid excessive effective leverage of the position.

The futures grid bot opens and closes positions within a specified price range, executing trades automatically.\n\nThe bot divides orders across multiple grids, entering and exiting positions at optimal prices, continuously arbitraging as the market fluctuates.

Profit from market fluctuations by buying low and selling high.

For example, with a long bot:
• When the price drops to a grid’s buy level, the bot buys and sets a sell order above it.
• When the price rises to a grid’s sell level, the bot sells and sets a buy order below it.

Quickly create your bot with AI parameters.

• Price range: The bot buys low and sells high within this range. If the price moves beyond the range, the bot pauses.
• Grids quantity: The number of orders the bot places. More grids mean more trades.

Select the trading direction based on your market prediction.

• Long: Ideal for rising markets, with the bot opening a long position upon creation.
• Neutral: Suitable for range-bound markets.
• Short: Ideal for declining markets, with the bot opening a short position upon creation.
Make a profit when the price moves within a preset range. The bot will automatically buy low and sell high/sell high and buy low.

Health factor refers to the ratio of your supplied assets against your borrowed assets. It represents the risk level of your position in a lending protocol. A higher health factor corresponds to a better safety level for your collaterals.Beware that your assets could be liquidated if the health factor goes below 1. You can raise it by adding collaterals or repaying the borrowed assets.

Tailored for spot and simple options trading, offers manageable risk with ease. An ideal choice for quickly mastering the basics of the crypto markets and building a solid foundational understanding.

Different currencies are converted into US dollar value, allowing for the calculation of margins and risk assessment on an account-wide basis.\n\nThis mode enables the offset of profits and losses across various trading pairs, facilitates trading with floating profits.

OKX system uses a risk model to assess the overall risk of the investment portfolio, then determines the maintenance margin requirements.\n\nBy constructing a hedging portfolio of a certain size, either spot-derivatives or derivatives-derivatives, this mode can minimize risk exposure for positions and significantly reduce the maintenance margin.

Trading and risk calculations are performed in the selected margin currency, effectively isolating the risks associated with different trading crypto. This method also simplifies your calculation process.

The maximum amount a user can borrow is determined by the minimum value of the amount calculated by the balance of the leverage currency of the user\'s current account, the ladder loan limit of the parent account, and the ladder margin limit. Ladder margin limits are detailed in introduction of leverage borrowed currency position stalls
Different currencies act as collateral to each other, and the account level shares the margin, and the fund utilization rate is higher
According to the gradient gear, the corresponding initial margin and maintenance margin ratio are calculated
The margin is converted into a single currency pair
(Note: Spot/Leverage - derivative hedging approach. Do not lock user accounts Spot assets, flexible)
Different currencies act as collateral to each other, and the account level shares the margin, and the fund utilization rate is higher
Margin requirements are lower when the position is larger, the hedging structure is better, and there is an option hedging/spot hedging strategy

Maintain margin calculation The overall risk of the portfolio is assessed according to the risk model. In some scenarios, margin has obvious advantages (refer to the following document links for specific scenarios).
Support a variety of hedging methods, thereby reducing margin requirements, and further improve the utilization rate of funds
Hedging between different risk units is not supported
Higher margin calculation (relative to cross-currency) when the user has a smaller position, a unilateral position, and does not hold an option hedging/spot hedging strategy
One position may cause other positions to break out (included in the concept of cross-currency)
(Application: different currencies are used as margin assessment account risk, user orders, margin collection and other modules)

Application: Different currencies calculate the margin respectively according to the risk unit (considering the effect after hedging and deducting), and evaluate the overall account risk after summary, affecting the user order, margin collection and other modules
Different currencies act as collateral against each other
There may be a higher risk of local position blowout due to the holding structure of a single guaranteed gold coin pair
The account margin is converted into US dollars
Suitable for trading rules and understanding costs is relatively simple, suitable as a stepping stone to the cryptocurrency market
There is no concept of leverage and the risks are relatively manageable
Trading varieties, business lines are relatively simple and limited
Leverage, contract trading is not supported
Profit and loss hedging: Support the same settlement currency between profit and loss hedging, floating surplus can be further opened
Easy to isolate the risks of different currency pairs
Maintenance margin calculation is based on the gradient gear, according to the position size gradient increase, more gentle
The margin calculation is simple and the system processes the request quickly
When the position size is small, the maintenance margin value charged for low gear is relatively fixed
One position may cause other positions to be associated with a bust (included in the concept attribute of cross-currency)
Hedging mode: derivatives hedging, spot hedging (currency hedging mode /USDT hedging mode)
Distinguish the concept of risk unit, and calculate the risk separately for different trading varieties

The portfolio margin model evaluates account-level maintenance margin requirements by identifying the overall risk of the portfolio based on the risk model. Users can build a certain scale of hedging portfolio (spot - derivative/derivative - derivative), as far as possible to reduce the exposure to risk, to maintain a significant decline in margin, so as to obtain a higher utilization rate of funds.
(Note: BTC-USD risk unit calculates the risk of contracts of all business lines under BTC-USD, and finally summarizes and evaluates the account risk)
Risk hedging: Users can reduce maintenance margin requirements by constructing a risk-hedging portfolio

Before publishing a signal, ensure you complete these steps and adhere to the format requirements.

- Signal description: Trading pairs | Suggested leverage | Suggested investment | Risk level, and brief introduction (e.g., BTC/USDT | 3x | 1,000 USDT | Risk level: Medium. This signal focuses on ...)

Display rules:
• Runtime > 10 minutes.
• Profit and loss percentage (PnL%) ≥ 0% (doesn’t apply to lead bots).
• Investment amount > 50 USDT (only applies to lead bots).
• For futures-based bots, the leverage must be ≤ 20x.
• Ranking refreshes every hour.

Featured, the sorting option, is based on a weighted calculation of bot’s PnL%, investment amount, risk, and bot traders’ PnL. For Lead bots, the profit-sharing ratio is also considered.

By default, all of your bots are shared in the marketplace. You can turn it off in your trade settings. However, please note that any bots you copy from the marketplace will not be ranked.

Please be aware that there may be some delay in following the lead bot creator’s actions due to market fluctuations.

抄底宝是的一种零手续费的策略交易方式，如果你想低于市场价格抄底买入一定数量的代币，但同时又担心挂单价格较低无法成交。此时，使用抄底宝策略锁定抄底，到期后可以保证你以挂单价格买入锁定数量一定比例或全部的数字资产。
高位出货，保证成交，零手续费
逃顶宝是的一种零手续费的策略交易方式，如果你想高于现在的市场价格卖出一定数量的代币，但同时又担心挂单价格较高无法成交。此时，使用逃顶宝策略锁定高价卖出，到期后可以保证你以挂单价格卖出锁定数量一定比例或全部的数字资产。

Open spread = (Average entry price of short futures – Average buy price of spot) × Open position size of futures.\n\nIf risk control triggers an auto-deleveraging, the close spread is also included.
Spot and futures opening fees; upon closing the position, spot and futures closing fees are also included. If staking is enabled, the fee for unstaking to spot is also included.
Total APY = Arbitrage APY + Staking APY (if any)
Total PnL = Arbitrage PnL + Staking PnL (if any) + Open spread + Trading fee + Borrowing interest (if any)
Expected return under current close conditions:

Close spread rate = (Spot average sell price − Futures average buy price) / Futures average buy price × 100%\nThe higher the rate, the greater the potential return.\nWhen closing a position, the system will monitor the market in real time. The close order will only be executed when the spread rate reaches or exceeds your set value, helping you lock in profits or limit losses.

Smart arbitrage is a spot-futures arbitrage strategy. It simultaneously holds spot and short futures positions, helping you earn funding fees with minimal risk.\n\nYou only need to set the investment amount to create the bot. Once the bot is running, it can steadily help you earn funding fees paid by long positions to short positions. This is especially useful in a bull market.
Positive arbitrage: When the 3-day cumulative funding rate is positive, use this bot by shorting futures to accumulate funding fees, while buying spot to hedge against price changes.

You can stop the spot-futures arbitrage bot at any time.\n \nWhen the arbitrage is no longer suitable for the current market conditions and the funding rate remains negative, you should stop the bot. Upon stop, we will automatically close short futures and sell spot at market price.
Staking rewards | Funding rate gains | Boosted returns

Your {cryptoPair} smart arbitrage aims to provide long-term and stable returns. If the maintenance margin ratio drops below 300%, the bot will automatically reduce both spot and futures positions by 10% each time, until the margin ratio returns to a safe level (≥ 300%).\n\nUnder normal market conditions, the risk of liquidation is extremely low.

If the maintenance margin ratio drops below {pctMinLimit}, the bot will automatically reduce both spot and futures positions by {pctReduce} each time, until the margin ratio returns to a safe level.\n\nUnder normal market conditions, the risk of liquidation is extremely low.

{termRsi} is a momentum indicator that gauges the speed of price movements. Generally, RSI uses 30 as the oversold threshold, 70 as the overbought threshold, which describes the price of an asset in relation to its fair value. \nA value lower than the oversold threshold suggests the short-term decline is coming to an end and a rebound could be imminent, so it would be ideal for starting a long position of futures grid. Vice versa, higher than overbought threshold is an opportunity for opening short position.

{termRsi} is a momentum indicator that gauges the speed of price movements. Generally, RSI uses 30 as the oversold threshold, which describes the price of an asset in relation to its fair value. A value lower than the oversold threshold suggests the short-term decline is coming to an end and a rebound could be imminent, so it would be ideal for starting a long position of infinity grid.

Total PnL is a floating value, consisting of grid profit and unpaired PnL.
Due to a drop in your fee tier, some of your grids may yield negative profits. You can adjust the grid quantity based on your current fee tier to prevent this.
Due to a drop in your fee tier, some of your grids may yield negative profits. You can adjust the grid quantity based on your current fee tier to prevent this.\n{viewMore}
Investments in the base currency are displayed in the quote currency of the pair.\nInvestments in other cryptos are displayed in that crypto.
Profit is calculated based on the market price of the quote currency.
When your grid count exceeds 300, the spot grid bot will use a dynamic order placement mechanism. It will place 150 to 300 orders near the current market price.
All your {crypto} are earning with Auto-earn.
All open orders will be canceled.\nThe bot will pause and stop placing orders until you resume it.

When resumed, the bot will reinitialize based on your current asset allocation and the latest market price.\n\nNew grid orders will be placed using your current base and quote assets. Some may execute instantly, resulting in immediate buying or selling.
Due to price changes while paused, {amt} {ccy} will be automatically added from your trading account to resume the bot.

All orders will be canceled, and the bot will pause — no trades will be placed until you resume.

During the pause, your assets will earn yield via Simple Earn.
When resumed, your selected Earn allocation method will apply automatically.
This feature is unavailable while the bot is paused.
Automatically earn extra rewards on your BTC.
To use Simple Earn allocation, lower the lower limit to {lowerLimit} or raise the upper limit to {upperLimit}.
After increasing your investment, Simple Earn allocation will be reset.

When the take-profit setting ({tpMode}) reaches {tpTrigger} or the stop-loss setting ({slMode}) hits {slTrigger}, the bot will stop.\nIf you set a delay ({delayTime}s), it stops bot after the trigger if the condition still holds. {ccy} will then return to your trading account.

If you don’t choose to reserve sufficient funds, the bot will only use the initial investment. If there are insufficient funds in your trading account when the grid trails downward, the trailing down will fail.
If you choose to reserve sufficient funds, the required funds for all orders during the trading period will be pre-allocated, ensuring no failures due to insufficient funds.

If your grid start condition isn’t Instant, the grid bot may not start immediately. Therefore, with the Trailing down option selected, funds can’t be reserved in advance.\n\nTo reserve sufficient funds, please set the start condition to Instant.
Includes the reserved funds for trailing down.
Modifying your grid settings will remove the Simple Earn allocation. Any earnings in Simple Earn will be redeemed and invested into your updated grid.
Modifying the parameters will reset the grid up and down, stop profit and stop loss settings you set.
Modifying grid parameters will remove your trailing up and trailing down settings.

The bot will reserve funds based on the initial market conditions, and the final funds used may differ from the reserved amount.\nIf the grid’s start condition is not Instant, funds cannot be reserved.

Modifying the parameters will reset the stop profit and stop loss you set.
Sell all the bot’s crypto to USDT at market price.

Instant: Immediately starts grid trading on bot creation

Price: After created, grid bot will be activated when trigger price is reached

RSI: Starts the bot when technical indicator triggered and other conditions are fulfilled

TradingView signal: Starts the bot upon receiving the relevant custom signal from TradingView

Insufficient balance. Transfer or deposit crypto.

If you select an active range like price -10 to +10, the system treats the values as absolute numbers and sets the range evenly above and below the market price.\n“Price” refers to the market price when the active range was last set or updated.

This is the part of your grid where active orders are placed based on current price movements.

Orders outside this range won’t be placed immediately. The related funds will be moved into Simple Earn and redeemed automatically when needed.

The active range adjusts dynamically with market changes, so funds are subscribed or redeemed in the background as prices move.

Earning optimization: Due to the current low APR of BTC Simple Earn, the system will subscribe to BTC Auto-earn instead to maximize your returns. Additionally, the gap from the current price for BTC orders will default to 100%.

Grid price range is the difference between your upper and lower price limits.

For example, if the lower limit is 1 and the upper limit is 5, the range is 4.
If you select an active region from price -25% to +25% of grid range, the region spans from price -1 to price +1, where price refers to the market price when the active region was last set or updated.

Due to the current low APR of BTC Simple Earn, the system will subscribe to BTC Auto-earn instead to maximize your returns. Additionally, the gap from the current price for BTC orders will default to 100%.

Earn extra earnings on your grid bot by putting idle funds to work through Simple Earn product.

Instead of placing all grid orders across the full price range, the bot keeps only the ones near the current market price active.

Funds allocated to price levels far from the market will be temporarily subscribed to Simple Earn, helping you earn passive income while those funds are idle.

3. Once reinvested, profits can’t be withdrawn.

When this is on, your bot will reinvest profits automatically, helping you grow your investment over time.

Note:
1. Reinvestment starts the day you turn on this setting.
2. Profits will only be reinvested if they’re enough to increase the per-grid investment.
3. Once reinvested, profits can’t be withdrawn.
Simple Earn allocation and the trailing feature can’t be turned on at the same time.
This is where your grid bot is expected to place orders based on the current market price.\n\nThe range adjusts automatically over time to stay aligned with market movements.
This feature isn’t available for this bot’s trading pair yet. Stay tuned!
Profit from each completed grid.\nTo accurately calculate the profit, the buy price of the bot’s first round of orders is based on the theoretical grid price.

Buy low, sell high, and earn grid profits.

• When the price drops to a grid’s buy price, the bot buys and sets a sell order above it.
• When the price rises to a grid’s sell price, the bot sells and sets a buy order below it.

Quickly create your bot with just 2 parameters.

• Price range: The bot buys low and sells high within this range. If the price moves beyond the range, the bot pauses.
• Grids quantity: The number of orders the bot places. More grids mean more trades.
You can manually stop the bot anytime.\n\nWhen stopped, all pending grid orders are canceled. All funds are returned to your trading account — you can check the details before stopping.

Trading on blockchains requires paying a network fee with native tokens such as ETH, BNB, OKT, MATIC, etc. When you don not have these tokens to make a transaction, you can quickly swap tokens you already hold for native tokens using the Swap for gas feature (it is only available for a few networks).\nGetting native tokens via Swap for gas also requires a network fee, which will be automatically factored into the amount you pay.\nYou will see an estimated number of general transactions when placing an order on Swap for gas. It refers to the number of token transfers you can make with your selected amount. The actual number may vary.

The estimated profit margin per grid after fee deduction.\n\nIf your fee tier drops, higher trading fees may cause some grids to yield negative. Additionally, the estimate is based on maker fees; in certain conditions, taker fees may apply.
Automated trading for effortless profit sharing
When the market price goes beyond a grid bot’s price range, the bot will automatically extend its range to stay aligned with market trends and better use your capital.

If the market price falls below the grid bot’s lower limit, the bot will place a new buy order below the market price, extending the bottom grid downward. To avoid over-investment, set a trailing down limit.

If the market price goes above the grid bot’s upper limit, the bot will cancel the buy order at the lowest price and place a new sell order above the market price, moving the entire grid upward. The SL price and trailing down limit will also move up accordingly.

This indicator shows the ratio of cumulative data value between leverage quote currency (USDT) and underlying asset(e.g. BTC, ETH) over a given period of time.

The volume‑weighted average price (VWAP) is the average price over a set number of candles, weighted by trading volume. The length setting defines the period, so prices with higher volume within the period have a greater impact on the VWAP. It highlights where most recent trades have taken place and helps you assess the short‑term trend, potential support and resistance levels, and whether the current price is trading within a reasonable value range.

The index price is calculated as the weighted average of spot prices from multiple major exchanges, ensuring fair pricing and resistance to anomalous deviations from any single exchange. USDT-, USDC-, and crypto-margined contracts each reference the index denominated in their respective unit.
Please enter a number greater than 0
This indicator isn’t available for intervals shorter than 5 minutes
This indicator creates a channel around the price, indicating volatility and potential reversal points. UB and LB represent the upper and lower boundaries of the Bollinger Bands, respectively.

This indicator measures the relationship between buying and selling pressure, indicating market sentiment. BR represents the ratio of the current closing price to the lowest closing price over a specific period. AR represents the ratio of the highest close to the current close over a specific period.
Taker buy volume refers to the trading volume of taker buy orders. Taker sell volume refers to the trading volume of taker sell orders.
This indicator measures the relationship between the asset’s current price level and its average price level, indicating overbought or oversold conditions.

This indicator calculates the difference between two moving averages to identify trend direction and potential entry or exit points. DMA represents the current value of the moving average difference, while AMA represents the moving average of the DMA line.

This indicator measures the strength of a trend and potential trend reversal. PDI measures the strength of upward price movements. MDI measures the strength of downward price movements. ADX quantifies the strength of the overall trend. ADXR smoothens ADX. The ADXR was developed to compensate for the variance of excessive tops and bottoms.

This indicator measures the ease of price movement, indicating the strength of a trend. EMV represents the current value of the ease of movement, while MAEMV represents the moving average of the EMV line.
This indicator is a moving average that places more weight on recent prices. It is more sensitive to price changes compared to simple moving averages.

This indicator refers to the ratio of net long positions to net short positions among the top 5% of traders with the largest open position value.\n\nLong/short ratio (Positions) = Net long positions / Net short positions

This indicator refers to the ratio of accounts with net long positions to those with net short positions among the top 5% of traders with the largest open position value.\n\nLong/short ratio (Accounts) = Accounts with net long positions / Accounts with net short positions

This indicator refers to the ratio of accounts with net long positions to those with net short positions.\n\nLong/short ratio (Accounts) = Accounts with net long positions / Accounts with net short positions
Zoom in and out on the y-axis to display orders at various prices.

This indicator measures the difference between the current price and the price a certain number of periods ago, indicating the strength of a trend. MTM represents the current value of the momentum, while MAMTM represents the moving average of the MTM line.

This indicator measures the difference between two moving averages and their convergence or divergence, signaling potential trend changes. “Short period” and “long period” define the number of periods used for the short-term and long-term moving averages, respectively. “MA period” defines the period used to calculate the moving average of the MACD line. DIF represents the difference between the short-term and long-term moving averages, while DEA represents the moving average of the DIF line.
This indicator calculates the average price of a cryptocurrency over a specific period, providing a smooth trend line.

This is an indicator based on kernel smoothing that forms envelopes around price with integrated alerts from crosses between the price and envelope extremities.

Bandwidth controls the degree of smoothness of the envelopes, with higher values returning smoother results.
Multiplier controls the envelope width.
Lookback controls the number of past candlesticks used in the calculation.
Upper and Lower refer to the bands that mark the envelope boundaries.
This indicator refers to the total value of open positions for a certain trading pair.
This indicator predicts an asset’s price fluctuations. Volume represents the latest trading volume, while MAOBV represents the moving average of the OBV line.
This indicator is a trend-based indicator that provides potential entry and exit points. It aims to provide signals for market trend reversals.

This indicator calculates the ratio of rising periods to the total number of periods. It represents the balance between buying and selling pressures in the market.\nPSY > 50% suggests that buyers dominate the market, indicating bullish sentiment. PSY < 50% suggests that sellers have the upper hand, indicating bearish sentiment.
This indicator measures the speed and change of price movements, indicating overbought or oversold conditions.

This indicator measures the percentage change in price over a specific period, indicating the momentum of a trend. ROC represents the current value of the rate of change, while MAROC represents the moving average of the ROC line.

This indicator is a momentum oscillator that compares the closing price to the price range over a specific period, indicating overbought or oversold conditions. Unlike the standard KDJ, SKDJ applies a smoothing function to reduce noise and provide clearer signals. K represents the percentage of the current price relative to the highest price over a specific period. D represents the simple moving average of K.

This indicator measures the momentum of RSI, identifying overbought or oversold conditions more accurately. STOCHRSI represents the Stochastic calculation of RSI, while MASTOCHRSI represents the moving average of the STOCHRSI line.

This indicator is a momentum oscillator that compares the closing price to the price range over a specific period, indicating overbought or oversold conditions. K represents the percentage of the current price relative to the highest price over a specific period. D represents the simple moving average of K. J represents the percentage of the current price relative to the lowest price over a specific period.

The supertrend indicator uses Average True Range (ATR)-based bands to highlight market trends and possible reversals.\nIt measures volatility with an ATR Length and applies a multiplier to set band distance. Price closing above the upper band signals an uptrend (shown in the Uptrend color), while closing below the lower band signals a downtrend (shown in the Downtrend color). This helps traders quickly spot trend direction, dynamic stops, and potential breakouts.

This indicator is a momentum oscillator that filters out market noise and identifies trends. TRIX represents the current value of the triple exponential average, while MATRIX represents the moving average of the TRIX line.

This indicator measures the ratio of the volume of advancing trades to the volume of declining trades, providing insight into market sentiment. It focuses on volume data rather than the number of advancing or declining assets. VR represents the current value of the volatility ratio, while MAVR represents the moving average of the VR line.
This indicator represents the total number of cryptocurrency units traded over a specific period, indicating market activity.
This indicator measures overbought or oversold conditions by comparing the closing price to the high-low range over a specific period.

Based on market liquidity, OKX position tier rules set variable position limits for different leverages selected, and require different margin ratios. When a high leverage is selected, the maintenance margin may fail to cover the loss during liquidation under extremely volatile market conditions. Thus, OKX limits the maximum borrowing amount for high leverage. The higher the leverage, the lower the maximum borrowing amount. Therefore, when sufficient funds are available, a larger order can be placed instead if the leverage multiplier is appropriately lowered.
Funds in OKX loan pool are from OKX Savings. The maximum borrowing amount of a user is limited by loan pool, user account level, and margin position tiers.

Margin means borrowing. Borrow to long and take profit when the price increases, or borrow to short and take profit when the price decreases. Margin, or collateral is required for all margin trading at OKX.\n OKX offers different margin trading modes users can select based on their needs.

The margin required will be transferred from cross to isolated automatically when user opens a position. A long position uses the base crypto as margin, whereas a short position uses quote crypto. The remaining margin and realized PnL will be transferred back to cross account automatically when positions are closed.
Borrow limit is determined by account level.

Buyer: Max ([Settlement price – Strike price] / Settlement price, 0) × Contract multiplier × Amount \n Seller: Max ([Strike price – Settlement price) / Settlement price, 0) × Contract multiplier × Amount
Verify margin based on trading account rules when in margin trading or transfer borrowed crypto

Users can buy crypto with borrowings or transfer the borrowed crypto under multi-currency cross or portfolio margin mode (Make sure the auto-borrow is turned on). Liabilities will be displayed in cross assets and the available margin is calculated as the discounted USD value of all crypto in trading account.

User can start trading after transferring collateral assets manually under isolated quick margin mode. If a user has borrowed some crypto, then could experience a margin trading very similar to spot trading. Base and quote crypto are both available for collateral and incur loans.
Users can choose to go long or short underlying assets. All the assets and liabilities will be displayed in position. Margin can be either base or quote crypto.
Buyer: Max ([Settlement price – Strike price], 0) × Contract multiplier × Amount \n Seller: Max ([Strike price – Settlement price], 0) × Contract multiplier × Amount

This indicator shows the futures price, the spot index price, and the difference between them (the basis). \n\nBasis = Futures price – Spot index price.
Trading volume is the value of futures traded in a set amount of time. Open interest is the sum of long and short holdings of expiry and perpetual futures of a specific asset at a specific time.
Open interest is the sum of long and short holdings of expiry and perpetual futures of a specific asset at a specific time.
This indicator shows how the top 100 traders allocate their funds by measuring the average percentage of initial margin used for long and short positions.

The percentage of long and short positions held by the top 100 traders with open positions. A trader’s account is counted as either long or short based on its net position (Long position size – Short position size). An account counts as long if the net position is positive and short if it is negative.
Long account % = Accounts with net long positions / Total accounts with open positions Short account % = Accounts with net short positions / Total accounts with open positions
Long/short ratio = Long account % / Short account %
Borrowed long refers to the borrowed amount of the quote currency ({quoteccy}). Borrowed short refers to the borrowed amount of the base currency ({baseccy}).
Margin long/short ratio = Borrowed amount of the quote currency ({quoteccy}) / Borrowed amount of the base currency ({baseccy})

• Coverage scope: After claiming the voucher, reimbursement covers the loss (if any) from the first futures DCA bot created and closed during the voucher’s validity. The bot must not have had any parameter adjustments (such as adding positions or adjusting stop loss/take profit settings) and must result in a loss at stop. Losses from non-first-time creations or those not created through the campaign page are not covered.
• The actual reimbursement limit equals the face value of the claimed voucher.
• To be eligible for reimbursement, the bot must be stopped within 7 days after the campaign ends. If not stopped within this period, reimbursement will be based on the bot’s overall performance on the 7th day after the campaign ends.
• After review, the reimbursement will be credited to your trading account within 14 working days after the campaign ends.

• The voucher is valid for 8 hours after being claimed.
• The main/sub-accounts of the same user can claim the loss coverage voucher separately, but reimbursement will only be provided for one account.
• Eligible perpetual futures: BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, SOL/USDT.

Fixed TP/SL applies to a fixed quantity in the position. In this mode, the order quantity doesn\'t change with the position size. You can place more than one TP/SL order.

Position TP/SL applies to the entire position. In this mode, the order quantity changes with the position size. A market order will be executed when the trigger price is reached. You can only place one TP/SL order.\nHowever, the order will not be executed if the position size exceeds the maximum order quantity for a single market order, or if there’s insufficient margin when the order is triggered. {link}
Maintenance margin ratio (MMR) is a risk metric for your positions. The lower the MMR, the higher the risk. When the MMR reaches or drops below 100%, your positions will be reduced or liquidated.

Funding fee = Position value × Actual funding rate, position value is calculated using mark price If the funding rate is positive, long position traders pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders pay long position traders.

Trade USDT-margined perpetual contracts for new crypto before the spot listing. Go long or short to gain early exposure and profit from price fluctuations. As the contracts may be rebased, remember to check the latest announcements.

OKX pre-market trading allows you to trade crypto before the spot listing. You can contribute to price discovery by trading USDT-margined pre-market perpetual contracts. When crypto is listed for spot trading, pre-market perpetual contracts are converted into standard perpetual contracts, and your positions and profits are automatically carried over. Contracts may be rebased based on the latest total crypto supply.

If new crypto is listed for spot trading:
• Pre-market trading stops after the listing.
• Your pre-market contracts are converted into standard perpetual contracts for the same crypto. Your pre-market positions and trading data are automatically carried over.
• After the conversion is complete, our standard funding rate mechanism applies. You can continue holding your positions or trade the contracts as usual. For a detailed conversion timeline, refer to the crypto-specific official announcement.

If the crypto is not listed on OKX:
• Pre-market trading for this crypto ends and all contracts are settled.
• The settlement price is based on the crypto’s arithmetic average price over a period before the end of pre-market trading. Under special circumstances, such as low market liquidity, the settlement price may be set at a very small value. For detailed settlement rules, refer to the pre-market trading terms for each crypto.

• Pre-market trading start time: Refer to the crypto-specific official announcement from OKX.\n• Pre-market trading end time and conversion time: After the OKX spot listing of a new contract is confirmed, the pre-market trading end time and the conversion time to standard perpetual contracts are announced in advance. This normally happens within a few hours after the crypto is listed for spot trading.\n• Delisting time and settlement time: If the crypto is not ultimately listed for spot trading, the pre-market delisting and settlement times are announced in advance.

Pre-market perpetual prices are determined by market behavior and may not reflect the actual listing price of the new crypto. While pre-market perpetuals may reflect market expectations, the actual listing price can be influenced by other factors and may not directly correlate with pre-market perpetual prices.
Pre-market futures are usually delivered 3 hours after the crypto is listed on the spot market. The exact delivery time isn’t yet determined.
Trade tokens pre-release using USDT; prices are market-driven.
Once the crypto is listed in our spot markets, your pre-market positions will be carried over and converted to standard perpetual contracts.
After the conversion, you can trade your positions however you see fit, while enjoying the depth and liquidity of our standard perpetuals markets.
Check back soon for opportunities to participate in our new pre-market projects.

Pre-Market Trading allows users to trade futures contracts on tokens that have yet to be officially launched. These contracts, known as pre-market expiry futures, track the price of these pre-launched tokens and expire when the token is listed or undergoes a Token Generation Event (TGE).

The system will pre-freeze the funds required for the current order between the buyer and seller as a guarantee for the trade. By default, all sell orders should be delivered with the new tokens. However, the seller can choose to deliver the tokens or compensate with margin before the delivery time. The seller must ensure that their trading account holds the required amount of new crypto; otherwise, the trade will be canceled. The system will unfreeze the buyer’s funds and compensate the buyer with the seller’s frozen margin. Once the delivery is completed, the corresponding quantity of crypto will be transferred to the buyer’s trading account, and the buyer’s frozen funds will be transferred to the seller’s trading account after deducting the trading fee.
How do I complete the delivery?

If the listing of crypto is delayed, the delivery phase for pre-market trades will also be delayed. However, matched orders will remain valid, and a new delivery phase will be announced separately by OKX.
What if there is a delay in the token listing schedule?

While pre-market trading may reflect market expectations, the official listing price itself can be influenced by various other factors. It is important to note that both the pre-market price and the official listing price are ultimately determined by market forces, and there is not necessarily a direct correlation between the two.
Does Pre-Market Trading affect the initial listing price of tokens on OKX?

After arbitrage orders are placed, the orders will be tracked every Y seconds. If the orders are not completely filled, the remaining orders will be automatically canceled and the remaining unfilled orders will be placed again at the latest queuing price until they are completely filled; when the orders are manually canceled, the auto-tracking will be automatically terminated as well. You can set a range of Y seconds, ranging from 5 to 120s.

Place an order with a price of the X tick size deviated to the best bid/ask: When buying, it is X tick size higher than the best bid price; when selling, it is X tick size lower than the best ask price.\nX is the taker range, and it can be set from 1 to 50.

Place an order with a price of the X tick size deviated to the best bid/ask: When buying, it is X tick size lower than the best bid price; when selling, it is X tick size higher than the best ask price. X is the maker range, and it can be set from 1 to 10. Tick Auto-tracking to track orders.
Spread rate = |Last price of the sold pair – Last price of the purchased pair| / Last price of the purchased pair × 100%

When enabled, after the tracking price reaches Z% deviated from the initial order price, the tracking will be paused; when the tracking price does not deviate, tracking will start again. The range can be set from 0.01% to 10%.

The estimated price at which an open position will be liquidated. The actual liquidation price is determined when your maintenance margin ratio reaches 100% and your position is liquidated or reduced.

Price gap refers to the difference between liquidation price and mark price. A positive gap means the position will be liquidated when mark price goes up. A negative gap means the position will be liquidated when mark price goes down.

Turned on by default under Portfolio margin account mode. In Spot-Derivatives risk offset mode, your spot position will be included in the risk unit and be used for spot-derivatives Delta offset. This can help you to have a more reasonable maintenance margin and improve the utilization of funds.When Spot-Derivatives risk offset turns off, only Delta offset between derivative positions is allowed. This advanced tool uses Volume Profile principles to identify high-volume price zones and automatically generates a support and a resistance line. These lines mark areas where the market may stall, reverse, or consolidate, offering data-driven insights into price trends.

Support line: A price level where buyers tend to prevent further declines.
Resistance line: A price level where sellers tend to cap price rises.
Ticks per row: Specifies how many ticks are displayed in each row. Each tick represents the smallest price movement of the pair.
Medium risk: Current trigger threshold has a reasonable distance from the liquidation level

• Coverage scope: After claiming the voucher, reimbursement covers the loss (if any) from the first futures DCA bot created and closed during the voucher’s validity. The bot must not have had any parameter adjustments (such as adding positions or adjusting stop loss/take profit settings) and must result in a loss at stop. Losses from non-first-time creations or those not created through the campaign page are not covered.
• The actual reimbursement limit equals the face value of the claimed voucher.
• The bot eligible for reimbursement must be stopped within 14 days of creation. If it is not stopped within 14 days, no reimbursement will be provided.
• After review, the reimbursement will be credited to your trading account within 14 working days after the campaign ends.

The funding fee is charged every {timeInterval} hours starting from 16:00 (UTC) each day. Settlement takes up to 1 minute, and each contract is processed in milliseconds without interrupting trading. Current funding rate is determined at start of the period {preTime}. If the funding rate is positive, long position traders pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders pay long position traders.

The funding fee is charged every {timeInterval} hours. Settlement takes up to 1 minute, and each contract is processed in milliseconds without interrupting trading. Current funding rate is calculated based on the recent {timeInterval} hours. If the funding rate is positive, long position traders pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders pay long position traders.

The funding fee is charged every hour starting from 16:00( UTC) each day. Settlement takes up to 1 minute, and each contract is processed in milliseconds without interrupting trading. Current funding rate is determined at start of the period {preTime}. If the funding rate is positive, long position traders pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders pay long position traders.

The funding fee is charged every hour. Settlement takes up to 1 minute, and each contract is processed in milliseconds without interrupting trading. Current funding rate is calculated using the premium of each minute over the last hour. If the funding rate is positive, long position traders pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders pay long position traders.

The funding fee will be charged or paid out at {time} when this perpetual futures is delisted. If the funding rate is positive, long position traders will pay short position traders. If the rate is negative, short position traders will pay long position traders.\n\nFunding fee = Position value × Funding rate.

Scaled orders allow you to set a price range and place multiple limit sub-orders at the same time, using your chosen amount distribution method

The amount of each sub-order decreases with a constant difference as the price rises, meaning your last sub-order has the lowest amount and highest price. Use this distribution method to reduce the average price of your buy orders.

The amount of each sub-order decreases with a constant ratio as the price rises, meaning your last sub-order has the lowest amount and highest price. Use this distribution method to reduce the average price of your buy orders.

The amount of each sub-order increases with a constant ratio as the price rises, meaning your last sub-order has the highest amount and price. Use this distribution method to increase the average price of your sell orders.

The amount of each sub-order increases with a constant difference as the price rises, meaning your last sub-order has the highest amount and price. Use this distribution method to increase the average price of your sell orders.

When buying an asset: If the market price of the trading pair exceeds this price, the bot will stop placing split buy orders until the market price returns to or falls below it.\nWhen selling an asset: If the market price of the trading pair falls below this price, the bot will stop placing split sell orders until the market price returns to or exceeds it.

To ensure a balanced execution between time and price, it’ll adopt a moderate approach when placing the first order.\nFor instance, the first buy order will be placed at a price between the best bid and the best ask, the second order at the best bid, the third order at the second best bid, and so on.

To ensure execution at more favorable prices, it’ll adopt a more conservative approach when placing the first order.\nFor instance, the first buy order will be placed at the best bid price, the second order at the second best bid, the third order at the third best bid, and so on.

To ensure quick execution, it’ll adopt a more aggressive approach when placing the first order. \nFor instance, the first buy order will be placed at the best ask price, the second order at a price between the best bid and the best ask, the third order at the best bid, and so on.
Number of visible orders: The order quantity placed in the order book that is visible to the market. Any hidden orders that remain will transit to visibility after the visible order is executed.

{termRsi} is a momentum indicator that gauges the speed of price movements. Generally, RSI uses 30 as the oversold threshold and 70 as the overbought threshold. Oversold and overbought describe the price of an asset in relation to its fair value. \nA value lower than the oversold threshold suggests the short-term decline is coming to an end and a rebound could be imminent.

Break down large orders into smaller, more discreet trades by setting:

• Amount per order: The crypto amount per order.
• Number of visible orders: The number of orders placed at once.
• Order amount: The total amount to trade before the bot stops.

Scatter large orders across the order book to protect your trading strategy.

Like an iceberg — only a small portion of the total order is visible on the order book, while the majority remains hidden.

By splitting large trades into smaller ones, you can reduce market impact, avoid price slippage, and keep your positions discreet.

Verify if the adjusted equity (USD value) of the account meets the initial margin (USD value) requirement after the order is placed. If the order can still be placed when the required currency is insufficient, there will be a debt generated for that currency after the order is filled.

Verify if the adjusted equity (USD value) of the account meets the initial margin (USD value) requirement after the order is placed, and if the available currency balance of the account meets the initial margin (amount) requirement for the order.
Dear users, before we can let you upgrade to Multi-currency Margin, you are required to pass a quiz to make sure you understand proper risk management.

1. Can PnL hedging and margin offsetting be realized between the derivative positions of the same currency (index) in the portfolio margin mode? Margin offsetting means that only the margin related to the net risk level is charged.

2. You can use automatic and non-automatic borrowing with cross and isolated margin modes in the portfolio margin mode. What’s the risk control process for placing an order in the cross margin mode with automatic borrowing?
3. What are the conditions for partial liquidation of positions under the portfolio margin mode in cross margin mode?
4. If your margin is insufficient, can our platform reduce the risk of the portfolio margin mode by increasing your futures or perpetual swap position?

5. There are various risks of crypto options trading, including extreme market volatility, high leverage, equipment issues, and others. These risks may affect our platform’s functionality or lead to errors. Do you fully understand these risks and take full responsibility for any relevant losses?
The account’s adjusted equity (sum of all currency amounts in USD * discount rate) doesn’t meet the maintenance margin requirements in USD. I.e., adjusted equity < maintenance margin.

Verify if the account’s adjusted equity meets the initial margin requirements (both in USD) after the order is placed. It’s still possible to place orders if the required currency balance is insufficient. In this case, a debt in this currency is generated and certain interest is charged after the order is filled.

Verify if the account’s adjusted equity meets the initial margin requirements (both in USD) after the order is placed. Also verify if the account’s available currency balance meets the initial margin requirements in crypto.
Dear users, in order to better control the capital risk in the future, you need to pass the answers before you can upgrade to the portfolio margin mode

An approval grants certain permissions to a DApp (decentralized application) to manage the token.
With this approval, you can generate a signature offline without network fees and set a flexible approval period. It’s commonly known as a Permit2.
With this approval, you can generate a signature offline without network fees and set a flexible approval period. It’s commonly known as a Permit.
DApps have the permission to transfer your assets, so always verify the DApp before approving with a Permit. If you’d approved a malicious DApp, your assets could be stolen.

Multisender helps you make multiple transactions without importing your private keys into a third-party tool.

· One-to-Many: Send assets from one address to multiple addresses in a single transaction.\n· Many-to-One: Send assets from multiple addresses to a single address (including exchange address).\n· Many-to-Many: Customize and send multiple independent transactions with one signature.
· Select the network, crypto, and multisender type. Make sure your address has enough balance for network fees.\n· Select or paste addresses in bulk and set all transaction settings.
